pairs.question: Which organizations selected the All-Big Ten teams in 1941?
pairs.answer: The organizations selecting All-Big Ten teams in 1941 were the Associated Press (AP), United Press (UP), and International News Service (INS). The AP team was selected by conference coaches, the UP team by regional experts, and the INS team based on input from scouts and scribes from the conference region.

pairs.question: Name the five players who were unanimously selected to the first team by all three selectors in 1941.
pairs.answer: The five players selected on the first team by all three selectors in 1941 were: quarterback Billy Hillenbrand of Indiana; fullback Bob Westfall of Michigan; end Dave Schreiner of Wisconsin; and tackles Alf Bauman of Northwestern and Dick Wildung of Minnesota.

pairs.question: Who was the only Big Ten player selected in the first round of the 1942 NFL draft?
pairs.answer: Bob Westfall of Michigan was the only Big Ten player selected in the first round of the 1942 NFL draft.

pairs.question: Which three Big Ten players were also selected as consensus first-team players on the 1941 All-America team?
pairs.answer: The three Big Ten players selected as consensus first-team players on the 1941 All-America team were Bob Westfall of Michigan, Dick Wildung of Minnesota, and Bruce Smith of Minnesota. Bruce Smith also won the 1941 Heisman Trophy.

pairs.question: How many players from the 1941 Minnesota Golden Gophers football team were named to the first and second teams in 1941?
pairs.answer: Seven players from the 1941 Minnesota Golden Gophers football team were named to the first and second teams in 1941.

pairs.question: List the players from the 1941 Minnesota Golden Gophers football team who were named to the first or second teams in 1941.
pairs.answer: The seven players from the 1941 Minnesota Golden Gophers football team named to the first or second teams were: quarterback Bill Garnaas; halfbacks Bill Daley and Bruce Smith; end Bob Fitch; tackle Dick Wildung; and guards Butch Levy and Helge Pukema.

pairs.question: What award did Ohio State fullback Jack Graf win in 1941, and why?
pairs.answer: Ohio State fullback Jack Graf won the Chicago Tribune Silver Football award in 1941 as the conference's most valuable player, despite not being picked for the first team by any of the selectors.
